analysis.latency: Our parallel racing architecture achieved a mean response time of 3.2s, significantly outperforming the p95 latency of Baseline A (14.0s) and Baseline B (20.5s). The median response time of 3.2s demonstrates consistent performance for typical user interactions, while the p95 latency of 3.6s indicates robust performance even under adverse conditions.
analysis.reliability: Our system achieved a 100% success rate due to its deterministic fallback engine and parallel provider execution. In contrast, Baseline A failed on 20.0% of requests due to API errors and timeout conditions, while Baseline B achieved 84% success rate through sequential fallback mechanisms.
analysis.cost: Our system maintained an estimated cost of $0.000 per request by strategically prioritizing free-tier providers. This compares favorably to Baseline A ($0.002 per request) and Baseline B ($0.002 per request).
analysis.providers: 44.0% of successful requests were fulfilled by the Gemini API, 30.0% by the AIML API, 16.0% by Hugging Face models, and 4.0% by the local deterministic engine.
